scenarios.title: Web client sends an authenticated turn and renders the response
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the web client has a valid same-origin HttpOnly session cookie established by the spec 070 web login flow with the assistant.turn scope
When the user types a NL message and submits it
Then the client POSTs to /api/assistant/turn with credentials: "same-origin" and a fresh transport_message_id
And the response body is rendered: text, citations, and any disambig/confirm/capture controls
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api row (TP-073-09) drives the served PWA assistant route and the live assistant HTTP endpoint, asserting schema-rendered body/sources/controls; companion .spec.ts is a documentation stub (no browser-driver runner is wired into ./smackerel.sh test e2e)

scenarios.title: Shared mobile client uses generated types from the golden schema
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the shared mobile client is built for iPhone/iOS and Android with types generated from the spec 069 golden schema
When the wire schema is changed in a way that breaks compatibility
Then the shared mobile build fails at codegen before either mobile platform ships
And no incompatible iPhone/iOS or Android client ships to users
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: build/unit codegen and renderer canaries guard web plus shared mobile schema drift before runtime; TP-073-03 specifically asserts each surface emits a render-descriptor-v1 conforming descriptor deep-equal to the per-fixture golden under tests/fixtures/assistant_response_v1/ (cases: text_only, with_sources, disambiguation, confirm_accept_decline, capture_acknowledgement, error_retry, unknown_shape)

scenarios.title: Transient network failure retries with the same transport_message_id
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the client POSTs a turn and the request times out
When the client retries
Then the retry uses the SAME transport_message_id
And the server returns the same response (deduped)
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api retry row (TP-073-09 path) asserts the live server observes the same transport_message_id and returns the deduped response; shared-mobile retry parity rows (TP-073-14) cover iPhone/iOS and Android once the shared mobile runtime exists

scenarios.title: Disambiguation prompt renders and round-trips on web and mobile
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a turn returns an AssistantResponse with a disambiguation prompt
When the user picks choice 2 on web, iPhone/iOS, and Android in separate sessions
Then every client POSTs kind = "disambiguation" with the same disambiguation_ref shape
And the eventual response on every client matches the chosen scenario's invocation result
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web and shared mobile e2e-ui rows must post the same ref/index shape across web, iPhone/iOS, and Android

scenarios.title: Confirm card renders identically and round-trips
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a turn returns an AssistantResponse with a confirm card
When the user accepts the action
Then the side-effect-bearing path executes server-side
And web, iPhone/iOS, and Android render the post-confirm result with identical structure
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: e2e-ui row must prove side effects are server-owned and result structure matches on all three surfaces

scenarios.title: Capture-as-fallback acknowledgement is identical to other transports
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the server returns AssistantResponse with CaptureRoute = true
When the client renders the response
Then the "saved-as-idea" acknowledgement appears with the same shape and copy as Telegram, HTTP-test, and WhatsApp
And no client-side capture logic exists; the server's response alone drives the UI
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: integration row must compare Telegram, HTTP-test, WhatsApp, web, iPhone/iOS, and Android acknowledgement fixtures

scenarios.title: No client-side scenario logic exists
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the response shape does not include a recognized control variant
When the client renders it
Then the client falls back to rendering the text body
And the client does NOT branch on scenario id, action class, or transport_hint to decide affordances
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: unit guard must detect prohibited scenario/action/platform/transport branching in web and shared mobile renderers

scenarios.title: Closed-vocabulary transport_hint is honored
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the web client sends transport_hint = "web" and the shared mobile client sends transport_hint = "mobile" on iPhone/iOS and Android
When the server processes all three clients
Then the server-side closed-vocabulary check accepts both values
And neither value alters scenario selection or response shape
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: integration and e2e-api rows must prove transport hints do not alter behavior across web, iPhone/iOS, and Android

scenarios.title: Web client meets accessibility floor
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a screen reader is active on the web client
When the user submits a turn and the response arrives
Then the response area announces via an ARIA live region
And keyboard navigation reaches the composer, send button, disambig choices, and confirm buttons in a sensible order
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api row (TP-073-11) asserts the served PWA markup contains the aria-live response region, labelled composer, and deterministic tab/focus order across composer/send/disambig/confirm/retry controls; driver-based screen-reader announcement validation is deferred to a future browser-driver foundation spec

scenarios.title: Shared mobile client meets VoiceOver and TalkBack accessibility floor
scenarios.gherkin:
Given VoiceOver is enabled on iPhone/iOS and TalkBack is enabled on Android
When the user submits a turn and the response arrives
Then the response renders with semantic labels readable by both mobile assistive technologies
And interactive controls (disambig list, confirm buttons) are focusable and announce their purpose on both mobile platforms
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: shared mobile e2e-ui rows must verify VoiceOver, TalkBack, and cross-platform parity

scenarios.title: Missing backend base URL fails loud at build/start time
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the SST-derived backend base URL is unset for a client build
When the client is built or initialized
Then the build or initialization fails loud with a NO-DEFAULTS error naming the missing key
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: unit/build rows prove web, iPhone/iOS, and Android use no backend URL fallback
